title: Output became cheap; meaning became the bottleneck
formulation: AI lowers the cost of producing outputs, but does not automatically solve meaning, context, decision quality or actionability.
need: Phase 1 need: make living meaning manageable, portable and executable knowledge.
evidence: The formula 'AI made output cheap. Now meaning is the bottleneck' became a repeated organizing formula across the dialogue and concept artifacts.
assumptions: External audiences will also recognize output abundance as insufficient without meaning production discipline.
risks: May be too abstract or too broad for first public understanding.
validate: Target readers independently recognize the bottleneck and provide concrete cases or objections that map to meaning production.
falsify: Target readers understand AI-output abundance but do not see meaning as the central bottleneck, or consistently identify a different bottleneck.
normalization_note: no special normalization note

title: Organizations need information production engineering
formulation: Organizations need an engineering discipline for producing, validating, updating and reusing knowledge outputs, not only AI-assisted drafting.
need: Need for a system of living knowledge circulation and engineering-managed information production.
evidence: The discussion repeatedly contrasted IPaC with ordinary documents, content operations, knowledge management and project management.
assumptions: Organizations will feel the pain strongly enough to seek a new discipline or operating model.
risks: May overlap with existing fields and be perceived as repackaging knowledge management or content operations.
validate: Executives or operators identify the same gap and ask for a system beyond AI writing and knowledge storage.
falsify: Existing knowledge/content operations frameworks already satisfy the need in real organizational contexts.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Knowledge Clumps are more than document collections
formulation: A Knowledge Clump is a structured and emergent knowledge formation containing artifacts, context, decisions, trace, feedback and implicit meaning.
need: Need to preserve living meaning rather than just files.
evidence: The concept was repeatedly defined, used in artifacts and contrasted with folders, archives and frames.
assumptions: The distinction remains meaningful when moved into tooling and external explanation.
risks: Could become vague if metadata and lifecycle requirements are not operationalized.
validate: Another Project or agent can use the concept to reconstruct context and preserve relations better than a document folder.
falsify: The concept collapses into ordinary archive/folder behavior in practice.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Living knowledge requires use, feedback and rebuild
formulation: Knowledge becomes living when it circulates through use, receives feedback, is reviewed, and can update canon and trace.
need: Need for a system of living knowledge circulation.
evidence: Living Knowledge and knowledge lifecycle diagrams consistently include use, feedback, review and rebuild.
assumptions: Feedback and trace are sufficient minimum mechanisms for liveliness in the IPaC sense.
risks: Could be overextended into a quasi-biological metaphor if not kept operational.
validate: Reviewed feedback changes artifacts, decisions or projections while preserving trace.
falsify: Knowledge artifacts can remain equally useful without feedback/review/trace loops.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Living knowledge circulation is a better system boundary than document workflow
formulation: IPaC should be bounded as a system of living knowledge circulation, not document workflow.
need: Need to avoid reducing the project to document management.
evidence: The user explicitly rejected document workflow as too narrow; Phase 1 and Phase 4 stabilize the boundary.
assumptions: The living knowledge boundary can be communicated without becoming too abstract.
risks: Public wording may sound less familiar than document workflow or knowledge management.
validate: The boundary helps new agents and readers avoid wrong reductions.
falsify: Users consistently need document workflow framing to understand or use the system.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: IPaC can be understood as a specialized operating system
formulation: IPaC can be framed internally as a specialized operating system for information production as code.
need: Need for bootability, runtime, status, governance and continuity.
evidence: The SOS metaphor generated productive internal structures: boot, runtime, dashboard, status, recovery and mentor layer.
assumptions: The operating-system metaphor clarifies rather than distorts the project.
risks: May be too heavy or misleading for external audiences.
validate: The metaphor helps new Project boot and helps agents understand boundaries and state.
falsify: The metaphor causes confusion, overengineering or expectation of actual software OS functionality.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Bootability is essential for continuity
formulation: A project cannot be reliably moved across plans, Projects or agents by files alone; it needs boot sources, instructions, status recovery and recovery commands.
need: Need to port living knowledge without losing context.
evidence: Portation produced Mentor Layer, Boot Protocol, First Boot Message and recovery criteria.
assumptions: A disciplined boot pack can restore enough context for continuation.
risks: Boot packs may become too large or stale.
validate: A new Project produces correct current_status / execution_mode / one_next_action from the boot package.
falsify: New Project repeatedly starts from zero or misreads status despite boot sources.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: AI Mentor Layer can preserve intersubjective collaboration
formulation: An AI Mentor Layer can transmit the mode of work: intersubjectivity, anti-generic behavior, boot, handoff, execution pivot and role boundaries.
need: Need to transfer not only files but interaction mode.
evidence: AI Mentor Layer was defined, packaged and accepted as a continuity layer.
assumptions: A prompt and source package can preserve interaction mode across environments.
risks: Interaction quality may depend on accumulated conversation history and not fully transfer.
validate: New Project enters the intended role, avoids generic answers and maintains human-AI separation.
falsify: New Project remains generic or becomes directive despite Mentor Layer.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Intersubjectivity improves human-AI creative engineering
formulation: The most productive mode is creative intersubjectivity: human provides living intention, AI provides structure and operationalization.
need: Need to preserve living human sense while making it operational.
evidence: The dialogue produced coherent artifacts, schemes and decisions through this mode, and the user confirmed the mode as valuable.
assumptions: This mode is reproducible beyond this specific chat.
risks: May become personality-dependent or prompt-dependent.
validate: New Project or another AI reproduces the constructive collaboration pattern using the Mentor Layer.
falsify: Other environments cannot reproduce it and default to command-response or generic consulting.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Human subjecthood must remain non-transferable
formulation: AI can guide methodology, but the human must retain intent, authorship, strategic judgement and final acceptance.
need: Need to protect the living source of meaning.
evidence: The boundary was explicitly formulated in the AI Mentor Layer and Phase 4 invariants.
assumptions: Clear instructions can enforce this boundary in multi-agent workflows.
risks: As agents become more autonomous, role drift can occur.
validate: Agents consistently ask for human decisions where meaning/strategy is involved.
falsify: Agents start making strategic canon decisions or appropriating authorship.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Feedback must be captured as raw evidence before interpretation
formulation: Feedback must be preserved in raw form before interpretation, classification or canon impact.
need: Need for research discipline and reliable learning from environment.
evidence: Repeated rule: Raw first. Interpret later. Embedded in feedback discipline.
assumptions: Raw capture is feasible in ordinary work without excessive overhead.
risks: Over-capture may slow execution.
validate: Feedback entries can be traced from raw source to review findings and decisions.
falsify: Raw capture proves impractical or unnecessary for useful learning.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: No-signal is diagnostic evidence
formulation: Silence or weak response should be treated as diagnostic evidence, not failure.
need: Need to avoid emotional or premature interpretation of external response.
evidence: No-signal protocols were defined and repeatedly linked to hook/audience/channel/timing diagnosis.
assumptions: Exposure and context can be recorded enough to make no-signal meaningful.
risks: Silence may be overinterpreted without sufficient exposure.
validate: No-signal analysis improves next test design.
falsify: No-signal records lack enough context and do not guide any useful revision.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Review-before-canon prevents premature changes
formulation: Routing feedback through review and decision before canon update prevents opportunistic or emotional changes.
need: Need for governance of living knowledge evolution.
evidence: Established as a mature governance invariant: no canon update without review.
assumptions: Review can be lightweight enough not to block learning.
risks: If too heavy, review discipline can become bureaucracy.
validate: Reviewed changes improve traceability without slowing work excessively.
falsify: The route is consistently bypassed because it is too heavy or unhelpful.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: MVI is the correct first external validation form
formulation: The first field validation should be a Minimum Viable Icebreaker rather than full launch or complete public methodology release.
need: Need to test living knowledge in environment without overbuilding.
evidence: MVI emerged as execution pivot and was operationalized, but Phase 4 invariants now classify it as execution candidate, not architecture.
assumptions: A minimal external cycle can generate useful evidence.
risks: MVI may be too small to produce meaningful signals, or too complex if five channels are activated.
validate: First MVI produces raw feedback/no-signal evidence sufficient for review.
falsify: MVI produces no usable evidence despite adequate exposure and review.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Five-channel contour can operate as one resonance system
formulation: Multiple external channels can function as distinct projections of one Knowledge Clump and one feedback loop.
need: Need to connect external communication back to living knowledge rather than isolated marketing.
evidence: The five-channel contour was designed and integrated, but Phase 4 invariants explicitly classify specific channels as execution candidates.
assumptions: Channels can be coordinated without overload.
risks: Channel sprawl; execution overload; confusion between architecture and publication tactics.
validate: Coordinated channels return distinguishable but integrable feedback into the same review route.
falsify: Channels fragment attention and produce disconnected tasks rather than a unified knowledge loop.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Trusted reviewers may be the safest first validation path
formulation: The first validation may be better through selected trusted reviewers before public release.
need: Need for low-risk first contact with environment.
evidence: Trusted reviewers first was recommended as default in prior execution framing.
assumptions: Trusted reviewers can provide higher-quality signals than early public noise.
risks: Reviewer sample may be biased and not represent the intended audience.
validate: Reviewers provide concrete questions, objections or cases that improve the next projection.
falsify: Reviewer feedback is too polite, vague or irrelevant to guide the project.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle

title: Meaning Bottleneck is the right first public hook
formulation: The first external hook should be Meaning Bottleneck rather than full IPaC architecture or Knowledge Clump theory.
need: Need to make the concept accessible in first contact.
evidence: Meaning Bottleneck was repeatedly selected as first asset focus, but not yet externally validated.
assumptions: The phrase is understandable and strong enough to invite response.
risks: May be perceived as abstract or unclear.
validate: Readers understand the phrase and provide examples, questions or interest.
falsify: Readers consistently fail to understand or care about the phrase.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Specific publication channels are not architecture
formulation: Specific channels and launch mechanics are execution candidates, not architectural invariants.
need: Need to protect architecture from tactical overfitting.
evidence: The Phase 4 correction explicitly states not to discuss LinkedIn, Telegram, site anchor, dashboard, feedback package, 71/72/73 unless they are architecture invariants.
assumptions: The generic invariant can be expressed as projection/use/feedback without naming channels.
risks: Execution documents may continue to leak into architecture.
validate: Architecture artifact remains stable even if channels change.
falsify: Changing channels breaks core architecture, implying channels were part of architecture.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Artifacts must be bootable and portable
formulation: Every serious artifact should include enough orientation to be portable: README, MANIFEST, status, registers and primary documents.
need: Need to move knowledge across environments without losing context.
evidence: All major packages were structured this way and portation depended on it.
assumptions: The orientation layers remain useful and not excessive.
risks: Packaging overhead may become too high.
validate: New Project/agent can use the package without prior hidden context.
falsify: Packages remain incomprehensible or too cumbersome despite orientation layers.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Mermaid diagrams are core comprehension artifacts
formulation: Mermaid diagrams accelerate comprehension and should be treated as working artifacts, not decoration.
need: Need to make complex relationships graspable and portable.
evidence: The user repeatedly confirmed schemes are understood faster and requested Mermaid posters.
assumptions: Mermaid remains compatible with Obsidian/Git/LLM workflows.
risks: Diagrams may become outdated if not versioned with text.
validate: Diagrams help new Project/agent recover structure faster than prose alone.
falsify: Diagrams introduce confusion or drift from the text canon.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Obsidian/Canvas can support Knowledge Clump dynamics
formulation: Obsidian and Canvas can serve as visual front-end for Knowledge Clumps and their dynamic relations.
need: Need to see relations, not only read documents.
evidence: User installed and confirmed Obsidian/Canvas as aligned with Knowledge Clump visualization.
assumptions: The tool can scale with project complexity.
risks: Visual layer may become manual overhead or another archive.
validate: Canvas improves navigation, trace and conceptual recall in actual work.
falsify: Obsidian/Canvas becomes decorative or unused.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle

title: Git/GitHub can function as versioned knowledge memory
formulation: Git/GitHub can provide versioned memory, trace, collaboration and change discipline for IPaC artifacts.
need: Need for traceable knowledge evolution.
evidence: Git/GitHub were repeatedly selected as infrastructure with Obsidian, VS Code, Codex and MCP.
assumptions: Git conventions can be made usable for knowledge work.
risks: Git overhead may block writing or overwhelm non-technical use.
validate: Git trace helps recover decisions and changes without slowing work significantly.
falsify: Git is too burdensome or does not add meaningful trace value.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle

title: Codex/VS Code can serve as engineering execution layer
formulation: Codex in VS Code can turn conceptual artifacts into file structures, scripts, websites, automation and checks.
need: Need to move from knowledge artifacts to operational implementation.
evidence: VS Code + Codex + Git + Obsidian + MCP were identified as technical contour.
assumptions: Codex can execute methodically under clear prompts and guardrails.
risks: Agent may diagnose but not finish, or implement partial fixes.
validate: Codex produces controlled, reviewable artifacts consistent with the canon.
falsify: Codex repeatedly creates drift, partial work or unreviewable changes.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: MCP can become a connectivity layer
formulation: MCP may serve as a connection layer between tools, sources and agents in the IPaC environment.
need: Need for cross-tool continuity and automation.
evidence: MCP was named among the powerful technological elements but not yet validated in the IPaC setup.
assumptions: MCP adds more continuity than complexity.
risks: Security, complexity and premature automation.
validate: A concrete MCP use case improves handoff, source access or trace without creating fragility.
falsify: MCP creates more maintenance burden than value at current stage.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Hypothesis registry prevents confusion between evidence and desire
formulation: A hypothesis registry helps distinguish validated rules, partially supported assumptions, unresolved questions and speculative concepts.
need: Need for research discipline.
evidence: The Phase 3 request and artifact explicitly introduce hypothesis status discipline.
assumptions: The registry will be updated after evidence, not merely created once.
risks: Registry may become stale if not tied to review/trace.
validate: Field evidence results in status updates through the defined route.
falsify: Registry is not used or statuses drift without evidence.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation

title: Need and Concept artifacts stabilize portation
formulation: Extracted Need and Concept artifacts help new Project understand the foundation before action or architecture.
need: Need for bootable conceptual continuity.
evidence: The portation phase produced Phase 1 and Phase 2 as foundational artifacts.
assumptions: New Project will read and use these artifacts effectively.
risks: Too many foundation files may slow boot.
validate: New Project correctly reconstructs need/concept without starting from zero.
falsify: New Project ignores or misreads these artifacts.
normalization_note: no special normalization note

title: Execution expansion can postpone action
formulation: Further architecture or artifact production can become a way to postpone field contact unless constrained by execution need and invariant separation.
need: Need to avoid self-referential design growth.
evidence: After the growth phase, user explicitly accepted the need to shift from expansion to field experience; Phase 4 correction reinforces separation.
assumptions: Awareness plus constraints can prevent postponement.
risks: The system keeps producing meta-artifacts rather than testing hypotheses.
validate: The project moves to evidence-producing actions after the recovery artifacts.
falsify: The project continues generating artifacts without field evidence.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion; treated as guardrail: architecture/execution expansion can postpone action

title: Protein-silicon resonance describes the collaboration loop
formulation: Human and AI form a productive resonance: human impulse plus AI structural expansion plus human redirection.
need: Need to name the felt mode of intersubjective collaboration.
evidence: The metaphor was meaningful in the dialogue and helped articulate intersubjective dynamics.
assumptions: The metaphor clarifies without becoming pseudo-scientific.
risks: Can sound pseudo-scientific or distract from operational architecture.
validate: It helps internal orientation while staying clearly metaphorical.
falsify: It confuses readers or is mistaken for scientific claim.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion; quarantine: metaphor/speculation must not enter architecture core

title: Semantic superconductivity is possible in well-designed meaning systems
formulation: A system may reduce loss and friction in passing meaning through complexity.
need: Need to preserve meaning across complex transformations.
evidence: The metaphor appears in the physics-of-meaning line: barriers, portals, resonances, superconductivity.
assumptions: Meaning-loss reduction can eventually be operationalized.
risks: Highly attractive metaphor may be prematurely canonized.
validate: Operational metrics show reduced context loss, rework, misunderstanding or boot friction.
falsify: The metaphor cannot be translated into operational criteria.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion; quarantine: metaphor/speculation must not enter architecture core

title: Knowledge Clumps can behave like living systems
formulation: Knowledge Clumps may display living-system-like properties: growth, feedback response, forks, emergence and reconfiguration.
need: Need to understand living knowledge beyond static artifacts.
evidence: Lifecycle diagrams and living knowledge discussions support the metaphor.
assumptions: Living-system language can be operationalized without mystification.
risks: Can blur metaphor and system design.
validate: Operational signs such as reviewed feedback-driven change, forks and traceable adaptation appear.
falsify: The clump behaves only as a static archive despite the language.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion; quarantine: metaphor/speculation must not enter architecture core

title: Social self-organization can form around a Knowledge Clump
formulation: A Knowledge Clump entering social circulation may attract feedback, use, discussion, forks and community-level self-organization.
need: Need for living knowledge to interact with social environment.
evidence: Discussed as a possible result of social resonance and living knowledge circulation.
assumptions: The external field can self-organize around useful knowledge, not just consume content.
risks: Long-term social effect may be overestimated.
validate: External participants begin applying, discussing, forking or contributing to the Knowledge Clump.
falsify: External contact produces only passive consumption or no sustained engagement.
normalization_note: risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: The book should become a public canon spine
formulation: The IPaC book can serve as a long-form public canon spine for the concept, evidence and evolving argument.
need: Need for coherent public articulation.
evidence: The book repeatedly appeared as public canon / spine, but has not yet absorbed field evidence.
assumptions: Long-form book structure is the right public container.
risks: Writing the book too early may freeze untested assumptions.
validate: Field evidence clarifies chapters and the book helps stabilize public understanding.
falsify: Shorter artifacts or tools prove more effective than a book at this stage.
normalization_note: parked until MVP/MVI or field validation cycle; risk flag: requires explicit review before promotion

title: Mature/preliminary/speculative separation is necessary for architecture discipline
formulation: The project must distinguish mature, preliminary and speculative concepts to avoid contaminating architecture with attractive but untested ideas.
need: Need for research discipline and architecture stability.
evidence: Phase 2 and Phase 3 explicitly introduced maturity/status separation.
assumptions: Maturity labels will be respected in later work.
risks: Speculative concepts may still dominate because they are energizing.
validate: Architecture artifact includes only stable invariants and marks execution/speculative items separately.
falsify: Later documents mix metaphors, execution candidates and stable architecture without labels.
normalization_note: validated means artifact/internal validation, not external field validation
